source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/11_Audit F-up/240415-17 ISO 갱신심사 & 2,3G MDR 심사 - 3EC/MDR 보완/[2nd response TD by H&abyz] Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector (08 March 2024) pdf/A-TCF-5-157 Clinical Evaluation/A-TCF-5-118 Clinical Evaluation Report (rev.2).pdf

document.title: Clinical Evaluation Report for Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

sourceSummary.oneLine: Rev. 2 clinical evaluation report for H&abyz Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector models A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Used to support conformity assessment of safety, performance, benefit-risk, and undesirable side-effects under Regulation (EU) 2017/745 and MEDDEV 2.7/1.
keyPoints: The device is undergoing initial CE-marking and is classified as class IIb under MDR Annex VIII Rule 10.
The intended purpose is general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children, excluding infants and pregnant women.
The report concludes compliance with MDR GSPR 1, GSPR 2, and GSPR 8 based on literature review, safety and performance testing, risk management, and comparison with equivalent devices.
Equivalent CE-marked devices listed are DRX-1, CXDI-70C, and CXDI-31.
The next clinical evaluation update frequency is every year.

evidence.claim: The document is A-TCF-5-118 Clinical Evaluation Report, revision 2, dated March 8, 2024.
evidence.evidenceText: Technical Documentation Document No. A-TCF-5-118 Rev. No. 2 Clinical Evaluation Report Rev. Date March 8, 2024

evidence.claim: The evaluated device is Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector models A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW.
evidence.evidenceText: Product name: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Model name: A1717MCW / A1417MCW / F1417MCW

evidence.claim: The manufacturer is H&abyz Co., Ltd. in Yongin-si, Gyeonggi-do, Republic of Korea.
evidence.evidenceText: Name: H&abyz Co., Ltd. Address: 2-Dong, 41-16, Cheoinseong-ro, Namsa-eup, Cheoin-gu, Yongin-si, Gyeonggi-do, 17118, Republic of Korea

evidence.claim: The report was prepared in accordance with MEDDEV 2.7/1 and Regulation (EU) 2017/745 Annex XIV.
evidence.evidenceText: This report is made in accordance with the MEDDEV 2.7/1 and Regulation (EU) 2017/745, Annex XIV.

evidence.claim: The device is undergoing initial CE-marking.
evidence.evidenceText: Whether the device is being developed/ undergoing initial CE-marking/ is CE-marked This device is undergoing initial CE-marking

evidence.claim: The product was not placed on the market yet at the time of the report.
evidence.evidenceText: This product is not placed on the market yet.

evidence.claim: The intended purpose is general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children, replacing film-based radiographic diagnostic systems.
evidence.evidenceText: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector (model: A1717MCW / A1417MCW / F1417MCW) is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children. It is intended to replace film based radiographic diagnostic systems
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Intended Purpose

evidence.claim: The intended patient population is adults and children except infants and pregnant women.
evidence.evidenceText: Patient populations Adult and children except infants and pregnant women
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Patient Population

evidence.claim: The device is not to be used for mammography, infant and pregnant women, fluoroscopy, or angiography.
evidence.evidenceText: Not to be used for mammography. Not to be used for infant and pregnant women. Not to be used for fluoroscopy and angiography.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Contraindications

evidence.claim: The device is reusable.
evidence.evidenceText: Single use / reusable Reusable

evidence.claim: The device has no known side effects according to the report.
evidence.evidenceText: The A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW have no known side effects.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Side Effects

evidence.claim: The device is classified as MDR class IIb under Annex VIII Rule 10.
evidence.evidenceText: Devices specifically intended for recording of diagnostic images generated by X-ray radiation are classified as class IIb. Therefore, this device is classified as class IIb in accordance with the rule 10 of Annex VIII Regulation (EU) 2017/745.

evidence.claim: The report lists DRX-1, CXDI-70C, and CXDI-31 as CE-marked devices used for equivalence.
evidence.evidenceText: DRX-1 of Carestream Health (CE marked by NB 0086); CXDI-70C of Canon (CE marked by NB 0197); CXDI-31 of Canon (CE marked by NB 0197)

evidence.claim: The report concludes DRX-1, CXDI-70C, and CXDI-31 are equivalent to the Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector despite non-significant differences.
evidence.evidenceText: we concluded that the CE indicators ‘DRX-1’ and ‘CXDI-70C’ and ‘CXDI-31’ are equivalent to ‘Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector’, although our products are not exactly the same in appearance (see 1.1), Scintillator (1.6), etc.

evidence.claim: The benchmark previous generation device is ADD, currently available for sale on the European market.
evidence.evidenceText: The benchmark device, which is previous generations of the device of A1717MCW / A1417MCW / F1417MCW, is ADD. The ADD is currently available for sale on European market.
evidence.pageTarget: ADD Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

evidence.claim: The device uses indirect energy conversion.
evidence.evidenceText: These proposed devices are the detectors with indirect energy conversion.

evidence.claim: The listed technical specification includes a-Si TFT sensor, CsI scintillator, 140 um pixel pitch, 40 to 150 kV energy range, and 1Giga-bit Ethernet communication.
evidence.evidenceText: Sensor Type a-Si TFT Scintillator CsI Pixel pitch 140um Energy Range 40 ~ 150kV Communication 1Giga-bit Ethernet
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Technical Specification

evidence.claim: The listed IP range is IP56.
evidence.evidenceText: IP Range IP56

evidence.claim: The service life is approximately 5 years.
evidence.evidenceText: The A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW is designed for a service life of approximately 5 years.

evidence.claim: The report identifies clinical benefits of lower patient dose, higher quality images, and faster image acquisition time for digital flat panel radiography.
evidence.evidenceText: Clinical benefit 1 – Reduction in patient dose and high quality image; Clinical benefit 2 - Fast image acquisition time

evidence.claim: The report concludes the benefits outweigh the risks.
evidence.evidenceText: We have confirmed that the expected clinical benefit of Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector (model: A1717MCW / A1417MCW / F1417MCW) can be achieved through clinical data. The benefits of our device outweigh the risks.

evidence.claim: The clinical evaluation report update frequency is every year.
evidence.evidenceText: The frequency of updates for the clinical evaluation report is every year.

evidence.claim: The responsible evaluator Sunghyun Kim is identified as qualified according to MEDDEV 2.7/1 Rev. 4 section 6.4 requirements.
evidence.evidenceText: The evaluator, Sunghyun, Kim is a qualified person in accordance with the requirement, and his CV is attached in Appendix IV.

reviewFlags.reason: Source metadata authoritySignal is FDA, but the document content primarily supports EU MDR, CE-marking, MEDDEV, MDCG, and 3EC notified body review; FDA appears only as a recall search database.

Potential conflicting evidence in classification table: one row says the device is not intended for recording X-ray diagnostic images, while the Rule 10 conclusion classifies it as specifically intended for recording diagnostic images generated by X-ray radiation.
Potential conflicting A/D conversion values: device specification states 16 bits, while the equivalence table lists A/D Conversion as 14 bits for the device under clinical evaluation.

Some tables contain OCR or formatting artifacts, including duplicated item numbering, split words, and inconsistent standard references such as EN 62222-1-1:2015 where the standards list uses EN 62220-1-1:2015.
